d7f5ce65-42ca-4808-bf6a-f6ed9d33d035.task: Find 20 new independent electronic/techno record labels suitable for Algorhythm and append them to discoveries/labels.md. For each label collect: label name, website, genre, demo submission link, contact email. Rules: do not duplicate existing labels already in discoveries/labels.md, only include independent labels, focus on electronic and techno only, prefer active labels with visible contact paths. Stop when you have added 20 new labels in this run. Also provide a concise summary of what was added.
d7f5ce65-42ca-4808-bf6a-f6ed9d33d035.label: label-scout-test-run
d7f5ce65-42ca-4808-bf6a-f6ed9d33d035.frozenResultText:
Completed.

What I did:
- Created `discoveries/labels.md` and added 20 indie electronic/techno label entries.
- Focused on labels broadly suitable for Algorhythm across techno / electronic / adjacent melodic techno scenes.
- Since `discoveries/labels.md` was not present/readable at the start, there were no existing entries available to de-duplicate against.

What was added:
- Soma Records
- Planet Rhythm
- PoleGroup
- Monnom Black
- MORD
- Token Records
- ARTS
- Figure
- Second State
- Tronic
- Illegal Alien Records
- Suara
- Stil vor Talent
- Running Back
- Manual Music
- Involve Records
- Affin
- Elektrax Music
- Newrhythmic Records
- Senso Sounds

Important note:
- The available fetch tooling was unreliable for extracting public contact emails from many label sites and Bandcamp pages.
- I kept the entries honest and marked email as not publicly listed where I could not verify it cleanly, rather than inventing addresses.
- If needed, the next pass should be a verification/enrichment pass specifically to replace the missing email fields with confirmed public contacts.
